Scouting_Note: The consensus #1 pick with elite vision and passing -- expect heavy assist totals and multi-point games as a future PP1 driver. Shot volume and Corsi will be excellent at the NHL level.
William Nylander comp with a two-way transition game and elite timing; assists and multi-point games will come in bunches as a PP contributor with strong Corsi and TK/GV.
Stock_Note: Representing Sweden at senior Worlds — strong showing further cements top-2 status and makes McKenna vs. Stenberg the draft's central debate.

Scouting_Note: Elite-sized two-way D projected as a top-pair anchor; heavy shot from the point and proactive defensive instincts generate blocked shots and SOG with PP assist upside.
Stock_Note: Skating concerns have grown among scouts — stride described as 'stiff' at NHL pace. Still a top-6 pick but has fallen from top-3 buzz of pre-season.

Scouting_Note: Dynamic puck-carrying defenseman and top play-driver in the class; high shot volume and multi-point games from power play deployment make him a premier fantasy D-man.
Big-bodied Liiga D with a heavy point shot and elite PP one-timer; shot-happy tendency translates directly to SOG totals with puck-moving skill adding assist and Corsi value.
The draft's top center (84 OHL pts) combines top-six scoring with elite defensive instincts -- multi-point capacity and takeaway value project well to fantasy across all deployment situations.
Stock_Note: Now consensus #1 center in the class after 29G/55A regular season plus 26 pts in 15 OHL playoff games. Some boards have him as high as 3rd overall.

Scouting_Note: Explosive two-way center whose standout skill is pick-pocketing opponents in transition; trusted in all situations including PK. Takeaways and Corsi are the primary fantasy categories.
Stock_Note: Returned mid-season from lower-body injury and showed 10G/17 pts in 13 games. No long-term concern; elite two-way tools fully intact.

Scouting_Note: Elite-skating offensive D with a 20-goal OHL season; combination of shot generation and puck distribution make him a fantasy gold mine for SOG, assists, and Corsi from the point.
6-foot-5 Tom Wilson comp with surprisingly elite hands (34 OHL goals); hits and PIM accumulate naturally while net-front presence drives goals and multi-point games.
Stock_Note: INJURY: Broke his left clavicle March 3, requiring surgery. Missed OHL playoffs and U18 Worlds. Expected healthy for Michigan State in fall. Dropped from potential top-5 to ~10-15 range.

Scouting_Note: Cerebral two-way center who earned top-six and PK time in the SHL at 17; elite stick-checking and breakout skill produce TK/GV and Corsi alongside playmaking assists.
Stock_Note: Rose ~10 spots on multiple April boards. One of the youngest players seeing regular SHL top-six time and excelling. Dynasty ceiling is climbing.

Scouting_Note: Rare offensive D-man who also dominates 1-on-1 defensively; led WHL D-men in scoring (40 pts) plus 75 PIM. SOG, PIM, and multi-point production give him a unique fantasy profile.
Dynamic blueline creator who led Vancouver Giants in scoring (50 pts); high hockey IQ and PP deployment translate to power-play assists, strong SOG totals, and elite Corsi numbers.
Stock_Note: Returned from mid-season lower-body injury and led ALL defensemen at U18 Worlds with 6 points. Fully healthy; stock fully restored to first-round range.

Scouting_Note: 6-foot-4 Finnish power forward with an NHL-ready wrist shot who dominates slot and net-front areas; physical game generates hits naturally while goals and SOG are the core fantasy output.
Czech winger posted 34 OHL goals with a dangerous quick-release wrister; goals and SOG are the primary fantasy output with multi-point games coming from PP deployment.
6-foot-3 Tappara center (Anton Lundell comp) who screens, deflects, and generates rebounds for goals with heavy frame; PK deployment adds blocked shot value.
Stock_Note: Disappointing U18 Worlds for Finland deepened offensive questions scouts already had. Fell 8+ spots from a pre-season top-10 projection to late first round.

Scouting_Note: Lane Hutson comp with elite agility and the shiftiest hands of any D in the class; projects as a top-10 fantasy defenseman for assists, SOG, and possession metrics as a PP quarterback.
Stock_Note: INJURY CONCERN: Has not played since January 4. Missed rest of season and playoffs. Size (5'11", 157 lbs) combined with lengthy absence has some boards placing him outside Round 1 despite top-15 talent ceiling. Major draft-day risk.

Scouting_Note: Big (6-foot-4) Swedish D who excels in gap control and lane disruption; fantasy value concentrated in blocked shots, Corsi, and takeaways with limited but real assist upside.
Mid-range pick with sharp edgework, a powerful accurate shot, and strong hockey sense; projects as a reliable middle-six forward contributing goals, assists, and positive possession metrics.
Led Hlinka-Gretzky in scoring; 6-foot-2 frame allows puck protection and creativity -- balanced goal-scoring and playmaking produce multi-point games with SOG as a natural byproduct.
Stock_Note: Led Hlinka-Gretzky tournament in scoring. Stock remains firm.

Scouting_Note: 6-foot-3 defensive-zone specialist trusted on the PK at 17; dominant board battles and positioning produce hits, blocked shots, and takeaways -- offensive ceiling limits G/A upside.
Stock_Note: Rose to 8th among NA skaters on Central Scouting's final rankings — one of the biggest single-season climbs on the board. First-round lock; potential top-10.

Scouting_Note: Led OHL scoring as a rookie with elite vision and dangerous quick-release; assists and multi-point games are the dominant fantasy categories with strong shot volume.
WHL co-leading scorer (50 pts, 22G) with a catch-and-release shot that reads as NHL-caliber; goals and SOG are the primary fantasy drivers with multi-point game upside.
Stock_Note: Blazers swept in first round, Hurlbert had just 3 points in 4 playoff games. Scouts flagging pace-of-play concerns. Still a probable first-round pick but no longer a certainty.

Scouting_Note: Tappara's defensive-minded D projects as a top-four shutdown blueliner; elite positioning and stick-checking make him a Blk/TK/GV/Cor specialist -- fantasy value is in peripheral categories.
Phil Kessel comp who blazes the wing and fires with accuracy; elite skating and dangerous shot generation project him as a top-six winger. Goals and SOG are the core fantasy value.
Stock_Note: INJURY: Suffered 8-week lower-body injury mid-season. Returned for U18 Worlds (6 pts, Canada's best forward) which is helping rebuild stock. Dynasty value intact but fell from top-8 projection to ~20-30 range.

Scouting_Note: Big (6-foot-4) SHL D-man with high-IQ puck movement and elite breakout ability; fantasy value centers on Corsi, assists, and defensive stats rather than scoring.
Stock_Note: Fell 14 spots on Upside Hockey's April board. Scouts now profile him more as a shutdown D than a two-way offensive driver. Size coveted but offensive ceiling concerns are mounting.

Scouting_Note: NTDP top scorer with a precise heavy release and lightning hands; two-way offensive driver who produces in all situations. Goals, SOG, and multi-point games are the fantasy targets.
Stock_Note: The biggest riser in the entire 2026 class. Went from unranked/C-grade pre-season to top-10 lock. Led USA with 9 pts in 5 U18 Worlds games. Some scouts project him top-5. Dynasty value has skyrocketed.

Scouting_Note: Cerebral QMJHL playmaker (53 pts) with elite vision and puck manipulation; PLC Dubois comp. Assists and multi-point games driven by PP usage with TK/GV added from forechecking sequences.
Physically imposing (6-foot-4, 216 lbs) power forward with a heavy NHL-caliber shot; goals, SOG, hits, and PIM translate well to fantasy if his compete level matches his physical tools.
Stock_Note: Has fallen drastically — outside first round on multiple boards after missing significant time to injury and failing to live up to top-center-in-the-class pre-season billing. Malhotra, Carels, and others now clearly ahead.

Scouting_Note: Creative Swedish center built on playmaking and transition sequences; Elias Lindholm comp projects as a reliable two-way middle-six center with steady assists and positive possession.
6-foot-4 QMJHL power forward with a powerful one-timer and PP dominance; goals and SOG from power play deployment are the fantasy anchor with hits as a natural physical bonus.

The biggest U18 Worlds stock-booster not already on most dynasty lists: 5 goals in 5 games for Slovakia including a hat trick vs. Germany. Fast, competitive, defensively sound — scouts who doubted his offensive ceiling all season had their concerns erased at the tournament.
Stock_Note: U18 WORLDS BREAKOUT: Exploded with 5G/5GP for Slovakia, vaulting from projected rounds 3-5 to late first/early second round conversation. The defining riser from the U18 tournament. Add to dynasty watchlist immediately.

Stock_Note: One of the most dramatic single-season ascents in the class. Rose from C-grade pre-season (Round 4-5 projection) to Central Scouting's 17th-ranked NA skater. Set QMJHL records for points by a rookie defenseman (13G/68A). First-round candidate.
